ExportSettings.Notes: Snapshot export configuration. Enabled = enable snapshot exports. OutputDirectory = single directory (legacy, use OutputDirectories instead). OutputDirectories = array of directories to write snapshots to (supports local paths and UNC paths). FileNamePattern = filename template with {ServerName}, {Timestamp:format}, {SnapshotId} placeholders. ExportIntervals: IntervalMinutes = export every N minutes, OnAlertTrigger = export when alert is generated, OnDemand = allow manual export via API. Retention: MaxAgeHours = delete files older than N hours, MaxFileCount = keep only N most recent files, CompressionEnabled = compress JSON files to .gz after export.

Alerting.Notes: Alert distribution configuration. Enabled = enable alert distribution. Channels = array of alert delivery channels. Each channel: Type = SMS/Email/EventLog/File/WKMonitor, Enabled = enable this channel, MinSeverity = minimum severity (Informational/Warning/Critical) to send, Settings = channel-specific configuration. Throttling: Enabled = enable alert throttling/deduplication, MaxAlertsPerHour = global limit, WarningSuppressionMinutes/ErrorSuppressionMinutes/InformationalSuppressionMinutes = how long to suppress duplicate alerts by severity.

Channels.Notes: SMS alert channel via PSWin API. Enabled = enable SMS alerts. MinSeverity = minimum severity to send SMS. Settings: ApiUrl = SMS gateway endpoint, Client/Password = authentication, Sender = sender ID, Receivers = STRING with multiple phone numbers separated by comma (,) or semicolon (;) - example: "+4797188358,+4791234567" or "+4797188358;+4791234567", DefaultCountryCode = prefix for numbers without country code, MaxMessageLength = truncate messages to N characters.

Windows Event Log alert channel. Enabled = write alerts to Windows Event Log. MinSeverity = minimum severity to log. Settings: empty (uses default Application log). Alerts are written to Windows Event Viewer under Application log with source 'ServerMonitor'.

File-based alert logging. Enabled = write alerts to log file. MinSeverity = minimum severity to log. Settings: LogPath = file path with {Date} placeholder (replaced with yyyyMMdd format). All alerts at or above MinSeverity are appended to the file with timestamps.

Email alert channel via SMTP. Enabled = enable email alerts. MinSeverity = minimum severity to send email. Settings: SmtpServer/SmtpPort = SMTP server configuration, From = sender address (supports {ComputerName} placeholder), To = STRING with multiple email addresses separated by comma (,) or semicolon (;) - example: "user1@domain.com,user2@domain.com" or "user1@domain.com;user2@domain.com", EnableSsl = use SSL/TLS encryption, Username/Password = authentication (leave empty for anonymous/Windows auth).

WKMonitor integration (legacy monitoring system). Enabled = write alerts to WKMonitor file format. MinSeverity = minimum severity to send. Settings: ProductionPath = UNC path for production environment, TestPath = UNC path for test environment (selected based on DOTNET_ENVIRONMENT), ProgramName = program identifier in WKMonitor, ForceAll = send all alerts regardless of throttling (default: false = respect throttling).

Throttling.Notes: Alert throttling and deduplication. Enabled = enable throttling to prevent alert spam. MaxAlertsPerHour = global limit across all channels (prevents overwhelming recipients). WarningSuppressionMinutes = suppress duplicate Warning alerts for N minutes. ErrorSuppressionMinutes = suppress duplicate Error/Critical alerts for N minutes. InformationalSuppressionMinutes = suppress duplicate Informational alerts for N minutes. Duplicate detection normalizes alert messages (strips numeric values) to catch similar alerts.

General.Notes: General application settings. ServerName is always auto-detected from Environment.MachineName (system ComputerName). MonitoringEnabled controls all monitors. DataRetentionHours affects snapshot cleanup.

Runtime.Notes: Runtime control settings. AutoShutdownTime (HH:mm format) = scheduled shutdown. MaxRuntimeHours = max duration before auto-shutdown. TestTimeoutSeconds = exit after N seconds (for testing). ConfigReloadIntervalMinutes = check for config changes every N minutes. CommonAppsettingsFile = UNC path to common config file - if set, syncs to local appsettings.json when hash differs (falls back to local if UNC unreachable). DevMode = when true, skips CommonAppsettingsFile sync (useful for local development/testing). All null = run indefinitely.

RestApi.Notes: REST API configuration. Enabled = start HTTP server. Port = listening port. EnableSwagger = interactive API docs at /swagger. AutoConfigureFirewall = add firewall rule automatically. Example address: http://localhost:8999 (Swagger UI: http://localhost:8999/swagger).

ProcessorMonitoring.Notes: CPU monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = how often to check. Thresholds: WarningPercent/CriticalPercent trigger alerts if exceeded for SustainedDurationSeconds. PerCoreMonitoring = track each CPU core separately. TrackTopProcesses = number of top CPU-consuming processes to track. ProcessCacheRefreshSeconds = how often to refresh full process list (default 120s). ServiceMapRefreshMinutes = how often to refresh service-to-process mapping (default 10min). EnhancedProcessMetadata = enable detailed process info including command line, executable path, service association (default true).

MemoryMonitoring.Notes: Physical RAM monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. Thresholds: WarningPercent/CriticalPercent trigger alerts if exceeded for SustainedDurationSeconds. TrackTopProcesses = number of top memory-consuming processes to track. ProcessCacheRefreshSeconds = how often to refresh full process list (default 120s). ServiceMapRefreshMinutes = how often to refresh service-to-process mapping (default 10min). EnhancedProcessMetadata = enable detailed process info including command line, executable path, service association (default true).

VirtualMemoryMonitoring.Notes: Virtual memory (page file) monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. Thresholds: WarningPercent/CriticalPercent for virtual memory usage. SustainedDurationSeconds = how long threshold must be exceeded. ExcessivePagingRate = pages/sec threshold for paging activity alerts.

DiskUsageMonitoring.Notes: Disk I/O performance monitoring (queue length, response time). PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. DisksToMonitor = specific drives to monitor. Thresholds: MaxQueueLength/MaxResponseTimeMs trigger alerts if exceeded for SustainedDurationSeconds. Note: This is separate from DiskSpaceMonitoring.

DiskSpaceMonitoring.Notes: Disk space (free/used) monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. DisksToMonitor: ["*"] = auto-detect all local fixed drives, empty array [] = auto-detect all, or specify drives like ["C:", "D:"]. Thresholds: WarningPercent/CriticalPercent trigger alerts when usage exceeds. MinimumFreeSpaceGB = absolute minimum free space threshold (triggers Critical alert).

NetworkMonitoring.Notes: Network connectivity monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = how often to ping/check hosts. BaselineHosts = list of hosts to monitor. Each host can have CheckPing (ICMP), CheckDns (DNS resolution), PortsToCheck (TCP ports), and Thresholds (MaxPingMs, MaxPacketLossPercent, ConsecutiveFailuresBeforeAlert).

UptimeMonitoring.Notes: System uptime monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. UnexpectedRebootAlert = alert if system was rebooted unexpectedly (dirty shutdown detected). MinimumUptimeDaysWarning = alert if uptime is less than N days (indicates frequent reboots). MaximumUptimeDaysWarning = alert if uptime exceeds N days (indicates server needs reboot for updates).

WindowsUpdateMonitoring.Notes: Windows Update status monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency (default 3600 = 1 hour). Thresholds: MaxPendingSecurityUpdates/MaxPendingCriticalUpdates = alert if count exceeds. MaxDaysSinceLastUpdate = alert if last update was more than N days ago. Alerts: AlertOnPendingSecurityUpdates = enable security update alerts. AlertOnFailedInstallations = alert on failed update installations.

EventMonitoring.Notes: Windows Event Log monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = how often to check event logs. EventsToMonitor = array of events to watch. Each event: EventId = Windows event ID, Source/LogName = event source and log name, Level = severity (Informational/Warning/Error/Critical), MaxOccurrences = alert if event occurs more than N times in TimeWindowMinutes (MaxOccurrences = 0 means alert on any occurrence, i.e., if count > 0). TimeWindowMinutes = time window in minutes to look back for event occurrences. UseRealTimeHooks = true for Event ID 1074 (shutdown) to use real-time system hooks instead of polling.

EventsToMonitor.Description: System - Kernel-Power (system rebooted without cleanly shutting down)
Task Scheduler - Task failed to start
Task Scheduler - Task completed with errors
DCOM - Server did not register with DCOM
Services - Service terminated with service-specific error
Services - Boot-start or system-start driver failed to load
System - Low virtual memory condition detected

ScheduledTaskMonitoring.Notes: Windows Scheduled Task monitoring. PollingIntervalSeconds = check frequency. TasksToMonitor = array of tasks to monitor. TaskPath: specific path (\Folder\TaskName), wildcard (\Folder\*), or recursive (\** = all folders). FilterByUser: null = all users, "{CurrentUser}" = same user as ServerMonitor, or "DOMAIN\username" = specific user. AlertOnFailure = alert if task failed. AlertOnMissedRun = alert if task missed scheduled run or overdue. MaxMinutesSinceLastRun = alert if task hasn't run in N minutes (set to very large number or null to disable). AlertIfDisabled = alert if task is disabled.

TasksToMonitor.Description: All Tasks owned by the same user as the ServerMonitor tool
